components.name: glucose
components.evidence: CAJ...includes 10% of reducing sugars (glucose and fructose), phosphorous and amino acids.

components.name: fructose
components.evidence: CAJ...includes 10% of reducing sugars (glucose and fructose), phosphorous and amino acids.

components.name: sucrose
components.concentration: 1110 mg/100 mL (~11.1 g/L) in unclarified pressed CAJ
components.evidence: Sucrose: 1110 ± 6.08 mg/100 ml (unclarified pressed cashew apple juice)

components.name: amino acids (total organic nitrogen)
components.evidence: CAJ has low total nitrogen content (0.04% w/v) and requires an additional nitrogen supplementation.  ...includes 10% of reducing sugars (glucose and fructose), phosphorous and amino acids.

components.name: phosphate (phosphorus)
components.concentration: 0.8 mg P/100 mL (~8 mg/L) in unclarified pressed CAJ
components.evidence: CAJ...includes 10% of reducing sugars (glucose and fructose), phosphorous and amino acids. (concentration from composition ref PMC10084989: Phosphorus 0.8 ± 0.2 mg/100 ml)

components.name: thiamine (vitamin B1)
components.evidence: CAJ also contains B-vitamins, mainly thiamin, riboflavin, niacin and folic acid, which are beneficial to the streptococci cultivation.

components.name: riboflavin (vitamin B2)
components.evidence: CAJ also contains B-vitamins, mainly thiamin, riboflavin, niacin and folic acid, which are beneficial to the streptococci cultivation.

components.name: niacin (nicotinic acid / nicotinamide, vitamin B3)
components.evidence: CAJ also contains B-vitamins, mainly thiamin, riboflavin, niacin and folic acid, which are beneficial to the streptococci cultivation.

components.name: folic acid (vitamin B9)
components.evidence: CAJ also contains B-vitamins, mainly thiamin, riboflavin, niacin and folic acid, which are beneficial to the streptococci cultivation.

components.name: L-ascorbic acid (vitamin C)
components.concentration: present; ~289 mg/100 mL in pressed CAJ
components.evidence: A possible synergic redox-depolymerization mechanism provides by ascorbate presence in cashew apple juice possibly causes HA molecular weight reduction. (concentration from composition ref PMC10084989: Vitamin C 289.39 ± 19.49 mg/100 ml)

components.name: potassium
components.concentration: 244 mg/100 mL (~2.44 g/L) in unclarified pressed CAJ
components.evidence: Potassium: 244.00 ± 4.0 mg/100 ml (unclarified pressed cashew apple juice)

components.name: magnesium
components.concentration: 40.3 mg/100 mL (~0.40 g/L) in unclarified pressed CAJ
components.evidence: Magnesium: 40.30 ± 0.30 mg/100 ml (unclarified pressed cashew apple juice)

components.name: calcium
components.concentration: 19.9 mg/100 mL (~0.20 g/L) in unclarified pressed CAJ
components.evidence: Calcium: 19.90 ± 0.2 mg/100 ml (unclarified pressed cashew apple juice)

components.name: iron
components.concentration: 1.66 mg/100 mL (~16.6 mg/L) in unclarified pressed CAJ
components.evidence: Iron: 1.66 ± 0.06 mg/100 ml (unclarified pressed cashew apple juice)

components.name: zinc
components.concentration: 0.2 mg/100 mL (~2 mg/L) in unclarified pressed CAJ
components.evidence: Zinc: 0.2 ± 0.13 mg/100 ml (unclarified pressed cashew apple juice)

components.name: O2 (facultative, no forced aeration)
components.evidence: a 3 L BioFLO III fermenter...with...2 vvm aeration was used for aerobic trials; Erlenmeyer flasks provided natural (non-forced) aeration. This record is the non-aerated (no forced 2 vvm) condition.

media.base_medium: Crude/undiluted cashew apple juice (CAJ) from Anacardium occidentale L., used as sole cultivation medium without supplementation (no yeast extract), no forced aeration; UV-sterilized

sources_chased.what: Target paper PMC3958174 (Oliveira et al. 2014, Braz J Microbiol) full text
added: confirmed glucose, fructose, phosphorus, thiamine, riboflavin, folic acid, ascorbate presence

sources_chased.what: Cited CAJ composition ref Rocha et al. 2007 (Appl Biochem Biotechnol 136:185-194, doi:10.1007/s12010-007-9050-6)
sources_chased.how: Springer paywall (idp.springer.com redirect); abstract via PubMed 18478387 gives no numeric CAJ composition

sources_chased.what: Open CAJ composition study (unclarified pressed CAJ, PMC10084989)

media.notes: Crude CAJ is a natural fruit juice used undiluted with no defined-salt supplementation, so its 'recipe' is the juice's intrinsic composition. Sugars/phosphorus/amino-acids/B-vitamins/ascorbate are confirmed verbatim in PMC3958174; niacin and total-nitrogen were recovered from the same paper. The cited numeric composition source (Rocha 2007) is paywalled, so bulk cations (K, Mg, Ca) and trace metals (Fe, Zn) plus numeric sugar/vitamin values were taken from an open composition study of pressed CAJ (PMC10084989); absolute concentrations vary with cultivar/processing so confidence is medium. Sodium, Cu, Mn, Co, Mo not reported verbatim in an obtainable source and are therefore omitted rather than invented.
